title: capsella bursa-pastoris prionophylla
intro: Capsella bursa-pastoris prionophylla, also known as shepherd's purse, is a plant species within the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is widely distributed throughout the world and often grows in disturbed or cultivated areas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. Medicinally, shepherd's purse is valued for its ability to treat various health issues, including diarrhea, bleeding, and skin irritations, and it has also been traditionally used to support women's health during menstruation. Herbal preparations of shepherd's purse can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
